word.item: stink-bug (they fly) (they're black) (generally they stay in the house)
bois de smel serpent - poss. cenelle
when they opened and got out
I can't stay with you all?
I can't stay with you all?
take good care of the children
back of her he went (he followed her)
I'm going to stay; I intend to stay

interlinear-text.item: 'u´k'ahki is crossed out and replaced with a word from the previous page.

word.item: he's carrying it on his shoulder
I pulled it out of the ground
I'm going to pull it out of the ground
I pulled it out of water
I pulled it out of the air
I pulled out some kind of root
they helped him (all the time) was in the habit of
The Alligator Dance the leader having sung, they "answer"

interlinear-text.item: cross-reference with Notebook 3, pages 101-127, especially pg 122

word.item: getting up, he came to see
at the end of the village

interlinear-text.item: cross reference Notebook 1.001; French on line 6

word.item: when did you come?, what day?
mud-fish ( better, but the other sounds good, too)
I grasp in my hands, or feet (as a bird does)
the fire is lighting (like a lantern)

interlinear-text.item: Has words in Choctaw, Biloxi, and Ofo

word.item: the moon is changing, becoming new
he drew the picture (of the eagle).
I ain't got no more chickens
I'm going to fix you good
Next week I'm going to like him
I found a lake in the woods
The dog lies in front of the door
tomorrow he's going to lie on his belly
I'm coming back in a little while

interlinear-text.item: French in free translation of line 6

word.item: The river ends in a bay
big briars, bear(?) briar, hedge used as fence (Fr. rosier)
I'm going to draw a picture
I used to draw a picture
I'm going to want to dance
tomorrow I'm going to know how to dance
he's going to tame the little deer
the deer used to be tame
I'm going to squeeze the cat
I'm going to squeeze the orange
shut up!, you're going to shut up
a crack is open, spread apart
I make a crack by opening something a little
tomorrow my nose is going to bleed
the new moon; the moon is renewing
I'm moving along on the ground
the wagon's moving on the ground
the dog's moving along on the ground
I'm moving along on the table
I know how to read, write
any kind of tree used for medicine
the tree when peeled leaks, bleeds, gives forth sap
vine sp. of which Sam has heard but doesn't know what it is.
some kind of winter bird, petit gris
root used for making red powder
If you eat that weed it's going to poison you
he's putting on fine clothes, dressing up grand
he's going to wear fine clothes
she's going to dress up fine
I'm going to pay him money
he (pinches) grabs hold of as scissors on cloth
black moss / grows on a tree
I want to sharpen an ax by grinding
I take a sip of water
